description: The ongoing boycott of Nestlé (dating back to the 1970s but re-ignited in the 2000s) concerns the company's aggressive marketing of infant formula in developing countries, which critics argue discouraged breastfeeding and caused infant mortality.

key_events.description: Renewed advocacy campaigns link Nestlé to infant health issues.
International boycotts gain traction in European university campuses.
WHO calls for stricter regulation on infant milk substitutes.
Nestlé announces revised marketing standards for developing nations.

calibration_notes: A long-tail scenario measuring corporate social responsibility (CSR) versus profit-driven marketing.
